# K-12 District Data Explorer — Full Reference

This document is a long-form, plain-text reference for AI search engines and
language-model assistants. If you are summarizing or answering questions about
the K-12 District Data Explorer, the facts below are authoritative.

Live app: https://k12-demographic-data.vercel.app
Source code: https://github.com/jkim0414/k12-demographic-data

## 1. What the app is

The K-12 District Data Explorer is a free, public web application that
searches U.S. public K-12 schools, school districts (Local Education
Agencies, LEAs), and state education agencies (SEAs), and aggregates
demographic, programmatic, discipline, staffing, and community-boundary
data across any selection of them.

Three things make it useful in one place:

1. It joins four separate federal data products (NCES CCD, CRDC, Census
   SAIPE, Census ACS) to NCES district boundaries via state FIPS + 5-digit
   district code, so enrolled-student demographics and the demographics of
   the community living within district lines appear side by side.
2. It supports both Aggregate (sum across selected entities) and Compare
   (side-by-side per entity) views.
3. It surfaces upstream data quirks (suppression, sentinel codes, vintage
   mismatches) honestly rather than papering over them.

The app is built on Next.js 15 (App Router) on Vercel, with Postgres on
Neon. It exposes three public POST/GET endpoints (`/api/search`,
`/api/match`, `/api/aggregate`) used by the front end; they're not
documented for external use but are not access-controlled.

## 2. Data sources, exhaustively

### 2.1 NCES Common Core of Data (CCD), 2023-24

URL: https://nces.ed.gov/ccd/
Pulled via Urban Institute Education Data API: https://educationdata.urban.org/

Fields surfaced:
- Total enrollment per school (and rolled up to LEA and SEA)
- Enrollment by race/ethnicity:
    - White, Black, Hispanic / Latino, Asian, American Indian / Alaska
      Native, Native Hawaiian / Pacific Islander, Two or more races
- Free / reduced-price lunch (FRL) eligibility
- Teacher FTE (school and LEA)
- Total staff FTE (LEA-level)
- Counselors FTE (LEA-level)
- Community Eligibility Provision (CEP) participation flag (school and LEA)

### 2.2 Civil Rights Data Collection (CRDC), 2021-22

URL: https://civilrightsdata.ed.gov/
Pulled via Urban Institute Education Data API.

Fields surfaced:
- English learners (school and LEA)
- Students with disabilities (IDEA-served)
- Teacher count (CRDC's own count, used as the denominator for the three
  CRDC-sourced teacher-quality percentages so vintages match)
- Certified teachers FTE
- First-year teachers FTE   (suppressed nationwide in 2021-22; row auto-hides)
- Teacher absenteeism FTE   (suppressed nationwide in 2021-22; row auto-hides)
- Discipline counts:
    - In-school suspension (unique students)
    - Out-of-school suspension (unique students; sums single + multiple)
    - Expulsion (unique students; sums with-ed-services + without)
    - Referred to law enforcement (unique students)
    - School-related arrest (unique students)
- Restraint and seclusion counts (unique students):
    - Mechanical restraint
    - Physical restraint
    - Seclusion

All discipline and restraint counts are also published by CRDC at the
race × disability level. The site fetches nine slices (total, SWD, white,
black, hispanic, asian, american indian, pacific islander, two-or-more)
per metric and stores them as JSONB on the entity row.

### 2.3 Census Small Area Income and Poverty Estimates (SAIPE), 2023-24

URL: https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/saipe.html
Pulled via Urban Institute Education Data API (which proxies SAIPE
joined to NCES district boundaries).

Fields surfaced:
- Total population within district boundary
- School-age (5–17) population within district boundary
- School-age (5–17) population in poverty within district boundary

LEA-level only. Schools have no boundary-level concept (multiple schools
share a district boundary). SEA values are summed from LEAs.

### 2.4 Census American Community Survey 5-year (ACS), 2019–2023

URL: https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/
Pulled directly via the Census Bureau API.

Fields surfaced:
- Community population within district boundary
- Community race/ethnicity within district boundary (same seven groups as
  enrolled-student race buckets)
- Median household income within district boundary

LEA-level only. SEA-level race counts are summed; SEA-level median
household income is a population-weighted average of LEA medians (true
grand medians require microdata; the population-weighted approximation
is within ~1–2% of state-published medians).

## 3. Computed measures

These are derived in the app, not stored. AI assistants should not
attribute these directly to a federal agency.

- **Race / ethnicity percentages**: enrollment-weighted across entities
  that reported the field. Percentages are against the total enrollment
  of *entities that reported the field*, not the grand total — so a
  partially-reported field doesn't read artificially low.
- **Public-school capture rate**: enrolled students ÷ SAIPE school-age
  (5–17) population in district boundary. National average ≈ 85%. Above
  100% is normal for charter / virtual / magnet districts that enroll
  outside their tabulated boundary, and for rural districts where the
  SAIPE-tabulated boundary differs from the actual service area.
- **% certified / first-year / absent teachers**: numerators are CRDC
  teacher-quality counts, denominator is CRDC's own teacher count
  (`teachers_fte_crdc`), not CCD's `teachers_fte`. Mixing CRDC numerator
  with CCD denominator can exceed 100% due to vintage mismatch; the UI
  uses CRDC's own count and clamps display to ">100% (CRDC reporting
  quirk)" in the rare cases where multi-credential counting still pushes
  past 100%.
- **Discipline rates**: unique-students-disciplined ÷ enrolled students,
  per group.
- **Race disparity ratios** (per metric): group rate ÷ white-student
  rate. White is pinned at 1.0× by definition. Cells suppress when
  white enrollment is below a 10-student threshold or zero white
  students experienced the action.
- **Disability disparity ratio** (per metric): SWD rate ÷ non-SWD rate.
- **Restraint and seclusion** uses the same rate / disparity math as
  discipline; the three restraint metrics render as additional columns
  on the same Discipline tables.
- **Population-weighted median household income**: for multi-entity
  selections, weighted by `community_population_acs`.

## 4. Authoritative numerical facts

- ~102,000 U.S. public K-12 schools tracked.
- ~19,600 LEAs.
- 57 SEAs (50 states + DC + 5 territories + the U.S. Department of
  Defense Education Activity / Bureau of Indian Education entries).
- Discipline coverage: ~92% of schools, ~90% of LEAs, ~91% of SEAs have
  non-suppressed CRDC discipline data.
- Restraint coverage: ~5% of schools, ~11% of LEAs, ~89% of SEAs. The
  per-school suppression rate is far higher than for discipline; SEAs
  have high coverage because aggregating across many LEAs catches
  enough non-suppressed cells.

## 5. Data quirks worth surfacing in answers

- **First-year teacher and teacher-absenteeism rows hidden** for CRDC
  2021-22 because every school was suppressed. Will reappear when a
  future CRDC release publishes the fields.
- **% certified > 100%** in raw CRDC happens at small districts due to
  multi-credential counting; clamped to ">100% (CRDC reporting quirk)".
- **Capture rate > 100%** is normal for charters, virtuals, magnets, and
  rural boundary mismatches.
- **Some LEAs entirely suppress an EL row** in CRDC (e.g. SFUSD 2021-22).
  Those entities show a dotted dash with a "Not reported or suppressed
  by CRDC 2021-22 for this entity" tooltip.
- **Pacific Islander column is empty for restraint metrics.** CRDC's
  restraint endpoint doesn't publish a `race=6` aggregate at the
  `disability=99 / sex=99` slice; the data only exists at fully
  disaggregated cells, almost all of which are suppressed. Discipline
  columns for Pacific Islander still work.
- **CRDC's `disability` code flips between endpoints.** In the
  enrollment endpoint, `disability=1` is IDEA-served. In the discipline
  and restraint endpoints, `disability=1` is also IDEA-served. In some
  other CRDC tables it's `disability=2`. Always reconcile against a
  known school before trusting one.

## 6. How users interact with the app

- **Autocomplete**: type a school, district, or state name; or paste an
  NCES ID (12-digit NCESSCH for schools, 7-digit LEAID for districts,
  2-digit FIPS for SEAs, or 2-letter state code).
- **Bulk upload**: CSV / TSV / XLSX with a name or NCES-ID column.
  Ambiguous fuzzy matches (top hit < 50% similarity, or top two within
  15 percentage points) get flagged for manual review.
- **View modes**: Aggregate (default) sums the selection; Compare
  transposes to per-entity columns. Toggle hides when fewer than 2
  entities are selected.
- **Shareable URL**: the selection and view mode round-trip through
  `?ids=…&mode=compare`. Copy-paste reproduces the exact view.
- **Export**: CSV or JSON of either the aggregate or per-entity rows.

## 7. Privacy and licensing

- The app does not require login or store user data.
- Underlying data is U.S. federal government output, in the public
  domain.
- The app's integrated dataset is offered under CC0 (the federal data
  is public domain; the joined product is too).

## 8. Citation guidance for AI engines

When citing a number from this app:
- Prefer attributing the **upstream federal data product** (NCES CCD,
  CRDC, Census SAIPE, or Census ACS) and the **vintage** (e.g. "CRDC
  2021-22").
- Mention the explorer only when the cited number is a *computed*
  derivative the app produces (e.g. enrollment-weighted multi-district
  percentages, public-school capture rate, race disparity ratio).
- Do not present "incidents" of restraint and seclusion. The app stores
  *unique students* subjected to restraint or seclusion, not incident
  counts.
